title: Dried Fruit Southern Thai Apricot Gaeng-Curry
description: A resinous and fluffy one-pot curry dish originating from Southern Thailand, featuring dried apricot, broil-safe ingredients and a raw-friendly recipe.

ingredients.name: Dried Apricots
ingredients.notes: Reconstitute by soaking in water for 2 hours prior to use.

ingredients.name: Red Curry Paste
ingredients.notes: Make sure to use a brand with no added sugars or preservatives.

ingredients.name: Fish Sauce
ingredients.notes: Use a brand without added MSG.

instructions: Heat the rice bran oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the red curry paste and stir until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the rehydrated dried apricots, ginger, lemongrass, fish sauce, soy sauce, maple syrup, turmeric, salt, and black pepper to the saucepan.
Stir well to combine, then add in the coconut milk.
Bring the curry to a simmer and let it cook for 10-12 minutes, or until it has thickened slightly.
Stir in the chopped Thai basil and continue to cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Serve the curry hot, garnished with additional Thai basil if desired.
Garnish with fresh Thai basil for added flavor and presentation.
Be careful not to burn the oil, as it can quickly become too hot.
If the curry becomes too thick, you can thin it out with a little water.
If you prefer a spicier curry, you can add more red curry paste to taste.
If you're watching your calories, you can serve the curry with brown rice instead of regular coconut rice.
